message:
perf(core): conformal-prediction SLO-respecting SQLITE_BUSY retry budget

Replace the naive `busy_timeout` retry-until-it-trips loop on the
explicit-transaction commit and BEGIN paths with a distribution-free
conformal prediction cap: keep a per-connection ring buffer of the
most recent successful commit latencies; on each BUSY retry attempt,
compute the one-sided conformal upper bound at miscoverage α and
short-circuit further retries once `elapsed + q̂` exceeds the
user-configured SLO.

Three new connection-scoped PRAGMAs:

  * `fsqlite.retry_slo_ms = <ms>`  (default 0 = disabled / legacy)
  * `fsqlite.retry_slo_alpha = <f>` (default 0.05; range (0, 1))
  * `fsqlite.retry_calibration_window = <N>`
        (default 256; clamped to [8, 4096])

The conformal quantile uses the classical split-conformal
construction (Vovk, Gammerman, Shafer): with K i.i.d. samples and
miscoverage α, picking the ⌈(1 − α)(K + 1)⌉-th order statistic
guarantees that the next realization falls within the prediction
interval with probability ≥ 1 − α — a finite-sample, distribution-
free bound. Ring buffer < 8 samples → no cap (we keep retrying
under the legacy schedule).

Latency is recorded on every successful explicit-COMMIT (this is the
path the wired-in retry loops belong to). Autocommit fast-path
commits intentionally bypass the calibration ring; tail-latency
budgeting matters where the retry actually loops.

This is a *policy* change, not a correctness change. Retry budgets
control blocking, not isolation: BUSY surfaces identically whether
the legacy `busy_timeout` expires or the conformal cap trips, and
the MVCC visibility/SSI/WAL invariants are entirely untouched. With
`slo_ms = 0` (the default) the cap is inert and the legacy path
runs unchanged — no behavior change for existing workloads.

  * `begin_pager_txn_with_busy_timeout` — checks the conformal cap
    before each BUSY retry, returns BUSY immediately when projected
    total latency exceeds SLO.
  * `execute_commit_with_cx` — same check inside the COMMIT-time
    BUSY-retry loop; on success, records the wall-clock commit
    latency into the calibration ring.

  * 7 unit tests in `connection/conformal_retry.rs` covering the
    quantile math, ring-buffer truncation, α and window bounds,
    disabled-default invariant, and projected-latency cap.
  * 4 integration tests in `connection.rs::schema_loading_tests`
    covering PRAGMA round-trip, BUSY-storm short-circuit semantics,
    legacy preservation when disabled, and commit-latency feed.
  * All 11 new tests pass; full `fsqlite-core` release suite shows
    zero new failures (delta vs baseline: 0 newly-failing tests;
    pre-existing failures track flaky concurrent tests unrelated
    to retry policy).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
